title: System Prompts vs. User Prompts
concept: System prompts set the model's persona, rules, and safety boundaries; user prompts contain the specific task instructions and inputs.

exercises.prompt: What is the main difference between a system prompt and a user prompt?

options.text: The system prompt sets the rules, and the user prompt asks the question.
The user prompt sets the rules, and the system prompt asks the question.
They are the exact same thing under different names.

exercises.explain: The system prompt establishes the rules, tone, and boundaries, while the user prompt provides the specific query or task to run.

cards.title: Who Rules the AI?
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how AI chatbots stay polite, act like specific characters, or refuse to write malicious code? It's not magic—it's all thanks to a hidden set of rules called the system prompt!

cards.title: Behind the Scenes vs. On Stage
cards.body: A system prompt sets the foundational rules, tone, and safety boundaries for the AI. A user prompt is the specific message, question, or task sent by the person chatting with the AI. Together, they guide how the AI behaves and responds.

cards.title: The Actor and the Director
cards.body: Think of an actor in a play. The director gives the actor their backstory, character rules, and accent (System Prompt) before the show starts. Once the curtain opens, the audience asks the actor questions or gives them tasks (User Prompts). The actor responds, but must stay in character according to the director's rules!

cards.title: The Detective AI
cards.body:
Imagine we configure an AI:
• System Prompt: "You are a friendly 1920s detective. Always use old-school slang."
• User Prompt: "Explain how to bake a chocolate cake."
• AI Response: "Well, pal, first you scramble up some cocoa and sugar in a tin, see? Then you bake it hot!"

cards.title: Myth: The AI Only Sees Your Input
cards.body: People often think the AI only reads the messages they type. In reality, the AI reads both the hidden system prompt AND your user prompt at the same time to decide what to say next!

cards.title: Side-by-Side Comparison
cards.body: Look at how the two prompts work together to shape the final answer.

left.text: Set by the creator. Defines the rules: 'Be a pirate, speak in slang, keep answers short.'

right.text: Set by you. Asks the question: 'What is the capital of France?'

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is typically contained in a user prompt?

options.text: The user's direct question or input

exercises.explain: User prompts are the actual messages or questions written by the end-user during a chat session.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Everyday users can easily rewrite the AI's system prompt directly inside a standard chat window.
exercises.explain: Only developers or system administrators set system prompts. End-users only type user prompts in standard chat windows.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence: The rules that set the AI's personality are called the _______ prompt.
exercises.sentence: The rules that set the AI's personality are called the system prompt.
exercises.explain: System prompts are hidden instructions that define the AI's overall rules and personality.

exercises.prompt: Identify the option that represents a USER prompt instead of a system prompt:

options.text: Option A: 'You are a helpful translator.'
Option B: 'Never share passwords with the user.'
Option C: 'Can you write a poem about autumn?'

exercises.explain: Option A is a system prompt because it sets a persona. Option B is a system prompt setting safety rules. Option C is a typical user prompt asking a direct question.

exercises.prompt: Why do developers put safety rules and behavior guidelines in the system prompt rather than the user prompt?

options.text: To let users change the AI's core rules during a chat
To set permanent rules, safety limits, and traits that the user cannot easily alter
To speed up the internet connection of the AI chatbot
To allow the AI to search the web for external answers

exercises.explain: The system prompt is designed to set long-term rules, safety limits, and behavioral traits that the user cannot easily override or change during the conversation.
